name: saturn_swap
title: SaturnSwap saturn_swap CLOB — Contract Integration Spec
description: Neutral on-chain integration spec for the SaturnSwap central-limit-order-book validator. Two in-scope deployments: the current 1% validator and the optional legacy 4% run-off validator (same baked fee_address + authorize credential; only fee_percent + ref script differ). Aggregators read resting order UTxOs and build taker fills directly on-chain; no SaturnSwap API and no Dexter/AMM-pool abstraction. Field names are SaturnSwap's own. All amounts are base units.
verifiedAgainst: mainnet (Koios, read-only): 1% + 4% addresses, baked parameters, fee_percent (100 and 400), wire formats, script_data_hash recipe
the saturn_swap validator's on-chain behavior (both deployments)
on-chain reference filler txs (mainnet 1% + preprod 1% and 4%)

onChainProven: 1% reference script decoded from Koios: addresses + baked Address parameters (fee_address, authorize_address)
fee_percent = 100 baked into the 1% validator, fee_percent = 400 baked into the 4% run-off validator (shared fee_address + authorize credential)
the non-auth sell-asset fee taker-fill path (1% rate submitted + confirmed on-chain; 4% rate build+eval-proven, not yet submitted)
provenance.onChainProvenNonAuthFill: The non-auth (sell-asset fee) aggregator taker-fill path is PROVEN ON-CHAIN at the 1% rate. Mainnet: aea570815f2c3697873f4bef7e8aa8fa130ad4766ed627fd1349f647369e0eab (https://cexplorer.io/tx/aea570815f2c3697873f4bef7e8aa8fa130ad4766ed627fd1349f647369e0eab; block 13615420, valid_contract: True, signed with the taker key only — no authorize co-sign — 1% fee in the sell asset to the baked fee_address, the same inline PaymentDatum byte-identical on owner + fee outputs, self-computed script_data_hash accepted). Preprod full fill: 90ddbf29a847a08115ba4608a4fa9e951ef5d97a84f9a30aeaeeb9a3cbc0baad (https://preprod.cexplorer.io/tx/90ddbf29a847a08115ba4608a4fa9e951ef5d97a84f9a30aeaeeb9a3cbc0baad; block 4880554). Preprod partial/relist: fdf5cab313e0242c677d09bf2890ecb4393d365bddf4eebfea21ea1c48e548eb (https://preprod.cexplorer.io/tx/fdf5cab313e0242c677d09bf2890ecb4393d365bddf4eebfea21ea1c48e548eb; block 4880596).
provenance.onChainProvenNonAuthFill4pct: The 4% non-auth fee branch (legacy validator 1af84a9e...) is BUILD- AND UPLC-EVAL-PROVEN but NOT yet submitted on-chain: a non-auth fill against the mainnet FRENCHIE 4% order, fee output at the 4% rate (feePercentX100=400) to the shared fee_address, ex-units mem 331449 / steps 112716586. Its construction is byte-identical to the proven 1% path except the compiled fee_percent constant (400 vs 100) and the ref script; a live 4% submit is the only step not yet performed for that rate.

blueprint.title: orion/saturn-swap
blueprint.note: unappliedTemplateHash is the blueprint hash BEFORE applying the two Address params; it is never an on-chain address. fee_percent is compiled in (a constant), not an applied param.

validators.label: legacy-runoff
validators.note: OPTIONAL, in scope. Legacy 4% run-off book: orders from before the 1% cutover may still rest here and are fillable for extra depth. This validator bakes fee_percent=400, so its fee output MUST use feePercentX100=400 (the 1% recipe underpays 4x -> is_fee_paid_to_address false -> DENY). Same baked fee_address + authorize credential as the 1% deployment; only fee_percent and the ref script differ.

optional4pctRunoff.rule: OPTIONAL but IN SCOPE. Aggregators MAY fill these orders for extra depth. You MUST build the fee output at feePercentX100=400; a 1%-rate fee output underpays 4x and the validator DENIES the tx. The fee_address is identical to the 1% deployment's. Mixing 1% and 4% orders in one tx is allowed as long as each order's fee output uses its own fee_percent and its own per-order PaymentDatum (the shared fee_address does NOT coalesce them).

resolution.rule: Two in-scope deployments. Read the order UTxO's payment credential (script hash) and resolve fee_percent + ref script PER ORDER: 73990b71... -> 1% (feePercentX100=100, ref 0e16cd00...#0); 1af84a9e... -> 4% (feePercentX100=400, ref 86cdaeed...#0). Skip anything that resolves to neither. The fee_address is the same for both. Use the resolved order's own rate for its fee output and its own ref script to spend it.

discovery.scopeFilter: Scan the 1% address (payment credential 73990b71...) and, optionally for extra depth, the 4% run-off address (1af84a9e...). Tag each order with its resolved feePercentX100 + ref script. Skip any order whose payment credential is neither hash. If you fill a 4% order you MUST use its 4% rate.

datum.name: SwapDatum
datum.wireNote: SaturnSwap emits indefinite-length arrays (0x9f..0xff). Definite arrays decode to the same Plutus Data and are accepted; reproduce indefinite for byte-identical output.

fields.name: owner
fields.encoding: Constr0[payment_credential, stake_credential]; payment = VerificationKeyCredential(Constr0[bstr28]) or ScriptCredential(Constr1[bstr28]); stake = Some(Inline(cred))=Constr0[Constr0[Constr0[bstr28]]] or None=Constr1[]. All hashes 28 bytes. Copy verbatim when building owner output.

fields.name: output_reference
fields.note: relist-chain link; fresh orders carry sentinel tx_id=0x00 (single byte), ix=0. NOT the PaymentDatum tag for a normal fill.

fields.name: user_sell_amount
fields.desc: quantity of the order's BUY asset the taker delivers this fill; <= amount_buy; == amount_buy for full fill; < triggers relist split

fields.name: input_index
fields.desc: index of this order's input among the SPENDING inputs only (tx.inputs), after canonical sort (tx_id asc, then ix asc). Reference inputs (incl. the ref-script UTxO) and collateral are separate context fields and do NOT count toward this index.

fields.name: output_index
fields.desc: position of the owner-payment output (tagged with PaymentDatum) in tx.outputs. OUTPUTS ARE NOT LEDGER-SORTED — they keep author order, so this is simply where you place that output in the list you build.

fields.name: input_index
fields.desc: index of the order's input among the SPENDING inputs only (tx.inputs), canonical sort (tx_id then ix); reference inputs and collateral excluded — same scoping as SwapAction.input_index

paymentDatum.name: PaymentDatum
paymentDatum.usage: Inline datum on BOTH the owner-payment output AND the fee output. Defends against double satisfaction.
paymentDatum.critical: output_reference = the SPENT ORDER's OWN input ref (tx_id#ix being spent), NOT SwapDatum.output_reference. The validator binds it from own_input.

fee.formula: total_fee = new_swap_amount_sell * fee_percent_x100 / 10000 (integer division, rounds DOWN), where fee_percent_x100 is THIS order's deployment rate (100 for 73990b71..., 400 for 1af84a9e...).
fee.feePercentNote: fee_percent is compiled into each validator (100 in the 1% validator, 400 in the 4% run-off validator) and is NOT a datum field or applied parameter. Resolve it per order from the order's own address (see resolution.map); never assume 1%. A 4% order filled at the 1% rate underpays 4x and the validator DENIES.
fee.assetDetail: paid in (policy_id_sell, asset_name_sell); lovelace if sell asset is ADA, otherwise that token
fee.minUtxo: The fee output is a real UTxO and must satisfy min-UTxO (its inline PaymentDatum counts in the size). ADA-sell: output lovelace = max(total_fee, min_utxo) (~1.2 ADA at current params; top up small fees from taker funds or the ledger rejects with OutputTooSmallUTxO). Token-sell: total_fee tokens + min_utxo ADA. min_utxo = max(requested, ledger min-UTxO); compute the ledger min-UTxO from the live utxoCostPerByte.
fee.feeOutputDatum: PaymentDatum{spent order ref} (same as owner output)
fee.aggregatorPath: fees_paid (non-auth): aggregators do NOT hold the authorize key (identical across both deployments), so they ALWAYS pay the fee in the sell asset at the order's own deployment rate (1% or 4%)

units.footgun: #1 integration failure mode. amount_sell, amount_buy, and every value quantity are RAW base-unit integers. Decimals are NOT on chain. Do NOT assume decimals (not 6, not 0).
units.decimalsLookup: per-asset, out-of-band: Cardano Token Registry / Koios POST /asset_info -> token_registry_metadata.decimals; ADA = 6
units.buildInBaseUnits: Do all ratio/fee/min-utxo arithmetic in base units; divide by decimals only for display/pricing

scriptDataHash.standardBuilderNote: cardano-cli, lucid-evolution, Mesh, cardano-serialization-lib compute body key 11 automatically. The validator imposes no custom integrity-hash requirement.

handRolledRecipe.source: the live Conway script_data_hash recipe the ledger accepts (proven on-chain; see provenance.onChainProvenNonAuthFill)
handRolledRecipe.legacyPathToAvoid: a variant that tag24-wraps the cost model + uses an empty 0x80 datums array is rejected by the ledger (PPViewHashesDontMatch); do not use it.
handRolledRecipe.provenance: Both this script_data_hash recipe AND the takerFill sell-asset fee-output construction are on-chain-proven — see top-level provenance.onChainProvenNonAuthFill.

relist.doubleSatDefence: validator fails on zero or multiple continuation matches
relist.continuationMatch: by payment-credential equality (same script) AND continuation SwapDatum.output_reference == spent order's own input ref

continuationDatum.output_reference: = spent order's own input ref (relist-chain link)

relist.continuationValue: EXACTLY two value entries: ADA + the sell asset ONLY (value_has_asset_and_lovelace requires exactly two policy entries; any third asset denies). Holds >= corrected_new_amount_sell of the sell asset. For a NON-ADA-sell order the continuation lovelace must be >= the spent script UTxO's lovelace (min_utxo_goes_back_to_script -> owner_paid_enough_ada_with_min_utxo(swap_value, script_value, 0)): preserve the original bundled ADA, do NOT attach a smaller freshly-computed min-UTxO. ADA-sell uses the 2-ADA buffer logic in adaSellBuffer.

adaSellBuffer.appliesWhen: sell asset is ADA AND new_amount_sell > 2000000

cancel.signedByOwner: key-hash owner -> key hash in tx.extra_signatories; script owner -> an input from the same script credential
cancel.scriptOwnerReturn: if owner is a script, value must return to owner with PaymentDatum{spent order ref} and equal the spent UTxO value

ownerOutput.minAda: supplied by taker; for a full-fill non-ADA-sell order also satisfy owner_paid_enough_ada_with_min_utxo: lovelace(owner) >= amount_buy + lovelace(script_utxo). NOTE: amount_buy is added as RAW lovelace regardless of the buy asset, so a token->token FULL fill is generally infeasible (would need amount_buy-as-lovelace of ADA) and must be done as a partial fill (case a). The literal formula governs.
ownerOutput.assetNameConstraint: exactly one asset name under policy_id_buy in the owner output; must equal asset_name_buy

feeOutput.contains: >= total_fee of (policy_id_sell, asset_name_sell), where total_fee = new_swap_amount_sell * fee_percent_x100 / 10000 using THIS order's deployment rate (100 for 73990b71..., 400 for 1af84a9e...). Never assume 1% — a 4% order needs the 400 rate or the validator denies.
feeOutput.minUtxo: floor to min-UTxO: ADA-sell output = max(total_fee, min_utxo); token-sell output = total_fee tokens + min_utxo ADA (see fee.minUtxo)
feeOutput.inlineDatum: PaymentDatum{spent order ref} (same as owner output)

takerFill.spendOrder: via reference script (reference input = refScriptUtxo for that order); present inline datum. cardano-cli: --spending-tx-in-reference <refUtxo> POINTS AT the ref-script UTxO, with qualifiers --spending-plutus-script-v2, --spending-reference-tx-in-inline-datum-present, --spending-reference-tx-in-redeemer-file, --spending-reference-tx-in-execution-units (the --spending-reference-tx-in-* family are the qualifiers, NOT the ref-UTxO pointer).
takerFill.validity: if valid_before_time = Some(t): tx validity_range entirely before t (is_entirely_before). invalid_hereafter <= t is admissible ONLY because Conway's invalid_hereafter is an EXCLUSIVE upper bound; set strictly < t if your library emits an inclusive bound.
takerFill.indices: input_index = index over the canonically sorted SPENDING inputs only (tx.inputs; reference inputs and collateral excluded). output_index = the position of the owner-payment output in tx.outputs in AUTHOR ORDER — outputs are NOT ledger-sorted.
takerFill.batching: DexHunter's PRIMARY path. Filling MULTIPLE orders in one tx: one owner output AND one fee output PER order, each tagged with inline PaymentDatum{that order's own tx_id#ix}. NEVER coalesce fee outputs — value_paid_to_with_datum requires EXACTLY ONE output matching {address, PaymentDatum} (zero or >1 -> deny), so a merged fee output satisfies at most one order and the rest deny. Each spent order gets its own SwapAction redeemer with its own input_index/output_index. A batch MAY mix 1% and 4% orders: resolve each order's deployment, readFrom both ref-script UTxOs (0e16cd00...#0 for 1%, 86cdaeed...#0 for 4%) as needed, and build each order's fee output at ITS OWN fee_percent (100 or 400). The two deployments share the same fee_address, so the per-order distinct PaymentDatum is what keeps the fee outputs from coalescing. Mixing the rate up (a 4% order with a 1%-rate fee output) denies that order and the whole tx.

sources.onChain: the saturn_swap validator's on-chain behavior (1% deployment 73990b71...), verified against real on-chain order datums + redeemers
sources.proofs: the reference filler's on-chain taker-fill txs: mainnet 1% https://cexplorer.io/tx/aea570815f2c3697873f4bef7e8aa8fa130ad4766ed627fd1349f647369e0eab ; preprod 1% full https://preprod.cexplorer.io/tx/90ddbf29a847a08115ba4608a4fa9e951ef5d97a84f9a30aeaeeb9a3cbc0baad ; preprod 1% partial/relist https://preprod.cexplorer.io/tx/fdf5cab313e0242c677d09bf2890ecb4393d365bddf4eebfea21ea1c48e548eb (the 4% branch is build+eval-proven, not yet submitted — see provenance.onChainProvenNonAuthFill4pct)

openItems: Min-UTxO ADA on owner/relist outputs depends on live utxoCostPerByte; compute from current protocol params, do not hard-code.
valid_before_time is POSIX milliseconds; confirm builder slot<->POSIX conversion against current era SystemStart/slot-length when setting expiry-bounded fills.
